operations.value: In one small randomized, double-blind crossover trial of healthy adults, 200 mg/day of L-theanine for four weeks improved some sleep-quality subscales, including sleep latency and sleep disturbance, more than placebo; the total sleep-quality score did not improve significantly versus placebo, so the evidence remains preliminary.
operations.notes: The trial enrolled 30 healthy adults. Between-treatment comparisons favored L-theanine for PSQI sleep-latency, sleep-disturbance, and sleep-medication-use subscales, while the total PSQI difference showed only a non-significant trend. No apparent adverse events were reported during the intervention. This patch intentionally avoids a broad insomnia-treatment claim.

sources.title: Effects of L-Theanine Administration on Stress-Related Symptoms and Cognitive Functions in Healthy Adults: A Randomized Controlled Trial

notes: Second controlled enrichment-pipeline pilot. Adds one narrowly scoped, sourced sleep claim to the existing L-theanine compound record without changing dosage or safety guidance.
